Who is this for: Business analysts, marketing managers, content writers, project managers, sales representatives, customer service representatives, administrative assistants, executives, consultants, researchers
Team or Dept this is for: IT, Legal, Compliance, Human Resources, Marketing, Sales, Operations, Risk Management
Description: What happens to your data after you submit it to AI tools.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with using everyday digital tools such as email, web browsers, or workplace software, and an awareness that AI assistants (such as chatbots or writing tools) exist and are increasingly common in professional settings.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with submitting data to AI tools and explain how corporate information may be stored, processed, or shared beyond your organisation's control. You will also be able to apply practical strategies to protect sensitive data and recognise which types of information should never be entered into external AI systems.
